errors: The ELM implementation has an age threshold mismatch. The CPG specifies an age range of 30-85 years, but the ELM implementation uses an age range of 30-80 years. The LDL Cholesterol threshold of 190 mg/dL matches the CPG requirement. However, the age threshold discrepancy needs to be corrected to exactly match the CPG requirements.

The HbA1c Laboratory Test value in the ELM implementation (9 g%) does not match the value in the CPG requirements (>= 6.5%). The unit of measurement in the ELM implementation is also in grams (g%) whereas the CPG does not specify the unit, but based on standard medical practice, it should be a percentage (%).

The ELM implementation has a mismatch in the age threshold. The CPG specifies an age range of 65 to 75 years, but the ELM implementation checks for an age range of 55 to 75 years. The logical structure and value set references appear to correctly implement the CPG decision logic, but this age threshold discrepancy needs to be corrected.

The ELM implementation of the MeetsInclusionCriteria logic is incorrect. The CPG requires that all of the following must be true: Screening Age (age >= 40 years), Gender (female), and Mammogram (no procedure in past 1 year). However, the ELM implementation uses an OR operator instead of an AND operator. The correct implementation should be: [MeetsInclusionCriteria] = [Screening Age] AND [Gender] AND [Mammogram]. Additionally, the Mammogram logic in ELM is IsNull(ProcedureLookBack(...)), which means that if there is no mammogram procedure in the past 1 year, the result will be null, but the CPG requires that if there is no mammogram procedure in the past 1 year, the result should be true. The correct implementation should be: [Mammogram] = IsNull(ProcedureLookBack(...)) = false, or more simply, [Mammogram] = NOT ProcedureLookBack(...).
